A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN CHEROKEE COUNTY GEORGIA JUST AFTER 730 AM IN THE MORNING. ALTHOUGH THE TOTAL PATH WAS 3 MILES...THE TORNADO SKIPPED ALONG THE GROUND AS THE DAMAGE WAS SPORADIC. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED ALONG A PATH THAT STRETCHED 1.75 MILES NE OF WALESKA TO 4.5 MILES NE OF WALESKA. IN ADDITION TO THE DOWNED TREES...A FENCE NEAR THE STARTING POINT OF THE TORNADO WAS DAMAGED WHEN A LARGE TREE FELL ON IT.

POSSIBLE TORNADO TREES DOWN AND HOUSES DAMAGED NORTH OF DUBLIN. ONE HOUSE WAS DESTROYED OFF OF CLAXTON DAIRY ROAD JUST EAST OF THE DUBLIN COUNTY AIRPORT. IN THE HOLLY IHILLS SUBDIVISTION NUMEROUS TREES HAVE BEEN SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND FALLEN ON HOMES.

THE STAGE HEIGHT OF A USGS STREAM GAGE ON THE LITTLE TALLAPOOSA RIVER AT GEORGIA HIGHWAY 100 REACHED FLOOD STAGE OF 17 FEET. THE RIVER CREST AT 17.6 FEET. MINOR FLOODING OF WOODLANDS AND FIELDS NEAR THE RIVER OCCURRED.
SEVERAL CREEKS AND STREAMS IN THE SOUTH PORTION OF UPSON COUNTY FLOODED SEVERAL DIRT ROADS FOR A FEW HOURS.
SEVERAL TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN OVER THE EASTERN PORTION OF THE COUNTY.

THE EM REPORTED SCATTERED WIND DAMAGE FROM GREYS LANDING ROAD TO CHARLIE HART ROAD. A BOW ECHO THUNDERSTORM EVENT CAUSED NUMEROUS TREES TO BE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED IN SPORATIC INTERVALS IN SEVERAL LOCATIONS ALONG AN 11 MILE STRETCH. DAMAGE TO LIVESTOCK BUILDINGS AND A CENTER PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEM OCCURRED. AN UNANCHORED SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED AND DESTROYED. WIND GUSTS AROUND 70 MPH WERE ESTIMATED.

EM RELAYED A REPORT OF DAMAGE ON HWY 147 AND US HWY 1. APPROXIMATELY 6 TREES WERE DOWNED. DAMAGE TO A COUPLE OF OLD BARNS AND A CENTER PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEM WAS REPORTED. APPROXIMATLEY TWO MILES TO THE EAST NUMEROUS OTHER TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN.

*** 3 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 8 MILES NW OF GRIFFIN. DOZENS OF TREES WERE DOWNED...SOME DAMAGING HOMES AS THEY FELL. ONE HOME SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE. 3 PEOPLE WERE INJURED WHEN THEIR CARS WERE STRUCK BY OR RAN INTO DOWNED TREES. PATH LENGTH 0.94 MILES...ON THE GROUND FOR 2 MINUTES.
*** 3 INJ *** TREES DOWN ON 2 CARS ON HWY 92 WITH 3 PEOPLE INJURED. NUMEROUS OTHER TREES DOWN. AT LEAST 2 HOMES DAMAGED. OCCURRED BETWEEN VAUGHN RD AND JIM GOODSON RD NEAR HWY 92.

POSSIBLE TORNADO TRACK IN NORTHERN WHITFIELD COUNTY. EM REPORTS NUMEROUS TREES DOWNED...ONE ON A TRUCK...AND CHICKEN HOUSE DESTROYED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. SURVEY WILL BE CONDUCTED LATER.

TORNADO TRACK IN NORTHERN WHITFIELD COUNTY. NUMEROUS TREES DOWNED...ONE ON A TRUCK...CHICKEN HOUSE COLLAPSED AND ANOTHER OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED ALONG THE PATH. PATH LENTH WAS 3.4 MILES AND WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A WEAK ANTICYCLONIC TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EASTMAN...NEAR DODGE COUNTY HOSPITAL ON 5/14/14. NUMEROUS STRUCTURES SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE INCLUDING A NURSING HOME AND COMMERCIAL BUILDINGS. SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED AND DOWNED IN THIS AREA. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 1.75 MILES AND ABOUT 100 YARDS WIDE WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 80 MPH.
LIKELY TORNADO PRODUCED 2 MILE PATH OF DAMAGE WITH NUMEROUS TREES AND SOME POWER POLES DOWNED ALONG OR NEAR HISTORIC HOMER HIGHWAY.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE SOUTHWEST OF SHILOH AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST 5.9 MILES INTO NORTHWEST TALBOT AND SOUTHERN MERIWETHER COUNTIES. A HOME ON SKYLINE DR HAD ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF AND A LARGE SHED DESTROYED. A FEW OTHER HOMES HAD MINOR DAMAGE. APPROX 100 TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. MAX PATH WIDTH 200 YARDS.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 5 MILES WEST OF COLLEGE PARK AND TRAVELED 2.5 MILES BEFORE LIFTING 4 MILES WNW OF EAST POINT. DOZENS OF TREES ALONG THE PATH WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED WITH MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO SOME HOMES DUE TO TREES FALLING ON THEM. THE MAX PATH WIDTH WAS 75 YARDS.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE WEST OF EAST POINT AN TRAVELED 3 MILES BEFORE LIFTING 2.5 MILES NNW OF EAST POINT. DOZENS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH ALONG WITH MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO SOME HOMES DUE TO TREES FALLING ON THEM. THE MAX PATH WIDTH WAS 75 YARDS.
THERE WERE MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ON BEN HILL ROAD AND STONE ROAD. WIRES WERE REPORTED DOWN ON FAIRBURN ROAD.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES SSE OF THE VININGS AND TRAVELED 1 MILE BEFORE LIFTING 1 MILE SSE OF THE VININGS. DOZENS OF TREES ALONG THE PATH WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE MAX PATH WIDTH WAS 75 YARDS.
NEWS MEDIA REPORTED A GUST OF 60 MPH MEASURED BY WEATHER EQUIPMENT AT THE LOVETT SCHOOL.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 95 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 8 MILES NW OF SANDY SPRINGS AND TRAVELED 1.25 MILES AFFECTING PORTIONS OF COBB AND FULTON COUNTIES. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH. DAMAGE TO HOMES DUE TO THESE TREES CAUSING MODERATE DAMAGE OF ROOFS. THE MAX PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS ALTHOUGH THERE WAS CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE OUTSIDE THIS PATH DUE TO STRAIGHTLINE WINDS WITHIN THE SQUALL LINE.
SEVERAL TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN ON TWO HOUSES. THE STREETS AFFECTED WERE HARRIS AND RUCKER ROADS.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES WEST OF ALPHARETTA AND TRAVELED 7.5 MILES THROUGH MILTON BEFORE LIFTING IN FORSYTH COUNTY. HUNDREDS OF TREES ALONG THIS PATH THAT WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. DAMAGE TO HOMES WAS DUE TO THESE TREES FALLING ON THEM WITH SOME MODERATE ROOF DAMAGE THE RESULT. THE MAX PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS ALTHOUGH THERE WAS CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE OUTSIDE THIS PATH DUE TO THE STRAIGHTLINE WINDS WITHIN THE SQUALL LINE.

OVER A DOZEN TREES REPORTED DOWN IN THE ALPHARETTA AREA. STREET LOCATIONS INCLUDE MAYFIELD ROAD...BETHANY ROAD...BETHANY BEND ROAD...AND CRABAPPLE ROAD. POWER LINES WERE ALSO REPORTED DOWN IN THE AREA.

A TORNADO CROSSED INTO STEWART COUNTY FROM ALABAMA AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ON A SEMI-CONTINUOUS 119 MILE PATH. THE TORNADO WAS AN EF-2 AT ITS PEAK...BUT CAUSED EF-1 DAMAGE THROUGH MOST OF ITS TRACK. TREES WERE DOWNED IN STEWART AND TALBOT COUNTIES BEFORE THE TORNADO STRENGTHENED INTO AN EF-2 IN UPSON COUNTY. IN A SUBDIVISION NORTH OF THOMASTON A RESIDENTIAL LEASING OFFICE WAS DESTROYED. THE TORNADO CAUSED EF-1 DAMAGE IN LAMAR AND BUTTS COUNTIES BEFORE LIFTING AS IT CROSSED INTO NEWTON COUNTY.

A MOBILE HOME WAS DEROOFED AND BLOWN 25 FT OFF ITS FOUNDATION ON PITTS RD.

A TORNADO FORMED ACROSS EXTREME SOUTHEAST HOUSTON COUNTY AND MOVED INTO FAR NORTHWEST PULASKI COUNTY. THE TORNADO PUSHED A DOUBLE WIDE TRAILER OFF ITS FOUNDATION ALONG PITTS ROAD...DESTROYED AN ABOVE GROUND POOL...DAMAGED MULTIPLE SHEDS...AND DOWNED MANY TREES AND POWER LINES.
THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF THE TORNADO DAMAGE THAT STARTED IN FAR EASTERN HOUSTON COUNTY.
TREES AND POWERLINES REPORTED DOWN ON ZEBULON RD.
TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN ALONG ZEBULON ROAD.
TREES DOWN ON HIGHWAY 83 SOUTH OF MONTICELLO.
TREES AND POWERLINES WERE REPORTED DOWN ALONG MACEDONIA CHURCH ROAD.
TREES REPORTED DOWN NEAR THE CLINTON AREA.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN COUNTY WIDE.
THIS IS A CONTINUATION OF THE TORNADO THAT FORMED IN ALABAMA AND MOVED INTO STEWART COUNTY AND NORTHEAST FOR GREATER THAN 100 MILES. NUMEROUS TREES IN THE AREA WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALL OVER THE COUNTY.
TREES AND POWERLINES WERE REPORTED DOWN ALL ACROSS THE COUNTY.
TREES AND POWERLINES WERE REPORTED DOWN OVER THE WESTERN PORTIONS OF THE COUNTY.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ACROSS WASHINGTON COUNTY.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS LAURENS COUNTY...SOME ROADS BLOCKED.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN WHEELER COUNTY.
TREES DOWN ACROSS JOHNSON COUNTY. AN OUTBUILDING WAS ALSO DESTROYED.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN TREUTLEN COUNTY.
TREES WERE UPROOTED AND A HOME HAD WINDOWS BLOWN OUT ALONG WITH OTHER DAMAGE.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ACROSS WARREN COUNTY.
MULTIPLE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN IN WRENS AND LOUISVILLE. ONE TREE FELL ON A HOUSE IN LOUISVILLE.
POWERLINES REPORTED DOWN IN EMANUEL COUNTY.

SEVERAL EMPLOYEES OF HENDERSONS GROCERY AND SHELL STATION NEAR HIGHWAY 36 REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL.

NUMEROUS TREES DOWN SSW OF DALTON. SOME TREES FELL ON POWER LINES.
TREE DOWN ON A STRUCTURE

MULTIPLE REPORTS HAVE BEEN RECEIVED FROM THE WHITFIELD COUNTY EM FROM AN AREA SW OF DALTON TO JUST SOUTH OF TOWN. THE FOLLOWING LOCATIONS HAD THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE WITH TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AND SOME DAMAGE TO A FIRE HOUSE NEAR DALTON.
911 CENTER REPORTED MANY TREES DOWN COUNTY WIDE. IN ADDITION THERE WAS A TREE DOWN ON A TRAILER NEAR ELM STREET AND A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF ON 3RD AVENUE.
NUMEROUS REPORTS OF TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS CHATSWORTH AND SOUTHWESTERN MURRAY COUNTY..WILL BE UPDATED AS REPORTS COME IN.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS MANY ROADS INUNDATED WITH WATER...AND SEVERAL CARS STRANDED ON THOSE ROADS.
WE RECEIVED A NOTE OF WATER FLOWING OVER THE ROAD ON LESTER SAUNDERS ROAD IN DODGE COUNTY VIA TWITTER FROM JEFF COX AT WGXA IN MACON.
